006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Utils.*;
021
022import java.util.*;
023import java.util.concurrent.*;
024import java.util.function.*;
025
026/**
027 * Stack-aware {@link BeanStore} overlay.
028 *
029 * <p>
030 * A {@code StackOverlay} delegates to a LIFO stack of {@link BeanStore} frames.  Each lookup walks the stack
031 * from top to bottom and returns the first match.  When the stack is empty the lookup returns
032 * {@link Optional#empty()} so that an outer chain (typically a {@link BasicBeanStore}'s {@code overridingParent}
033 * slot) can fall through to its remaining tiers.
034 *
035 * <p>
036 * This class is designed to live in the {@code overridingParent} slot of a {@link BasicBeanStore}.  Pushing a
037 * frame inserts a new tier-1 layer; popping it removes that layer.  Stack order is enforced LIFO &mdash; popping
038 * out of order or popping an empty stack throws {@link IllegalStateException}.
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * The implementation uses a {@link ConcurrentLinkedDeque} so that reads from concurrent HTTP request threads
042 * see a consistent view while pushes and pops are in flight.  Pushes/pops are expected to be infrequent (test
043 * setup/teardown); reads are the hot path.
044 *
045 * <p>
046 * <b>Composition, not inheritance.</b>  {@code StackOverlay} {@code implements} {@link BeanStore} rather than
047 * extending {@link BasicBeanStore} so that it does <i>not</i> inherit the entries / defaults / parent storage
048 * that a {@link BasicBeanStore} provides.  A {@code StackOverlay} is purely a stack of pluggable bean stores.
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
051 * <p class='bjava'>
052 *    <jc>// Create an overlay-capable bean store.</jc>
053 *    StackOverlay <jv>overlay</jv> = <jk>new</jk> StackOverlay();
054 *    BasicBeanStore <jv>store</jv> = <jk>new</jk> BasicBeanStore(<jk>null</jk>, <jv>overlay</jv>);
055 *
056 *    <jc>// Push a per-test override frame.</jc>
057 *    <jv>overlay</jv>.push(<jk>new</jk> BasicBeanStore().addBean(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <jv>mock</jv>));
058 *    <jv>store</jv>.getBean(MyService.<jk>class</jk>);  <jc>// returns the mock</jc>
059 *
060 *    <jc>// Pop it at end of test.</jc>
061 *    <jv>overlay</jv>.pop();
062 * </p>
063 *
064 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
065 *    <li class='jc'>{@link BasicBeanStore} - The bean store this overlay sits in front of.
066 * </ul>
067 *
068 * @since 10.0.0
069 */
070public final class StackOverlay implements BeanStore {
071
072   private final Deque<BeanStore> frames = new ConcurrentLinkedDeque<>();
073
074   /**
075    * Constructor.
076    */
077   public StackOverlay() { /* no-op */ }
078
079   /**
080    * Pushes a new overlay frame onto the top of the stack.
081    *
082    * <p>
083    * The frame becomes the highest-priority lookup target for subsequent {@link #getBean(Class)} /
084    * {@link #getBeanSupplier(Class)} calls until it is popped.
085    *
086    * @param overlay The bean store to push.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
087    * @return This object.
088    */
089   public StackOverlay push(BeanStore overlay) {
090      assertArgNotNull("overlay", overlay);
091      frames.push(overlay);
092      return this;
093   }
094
095   /**
096    * Pops the top frame off the stack.
097    *
098    * @return The popped frame.
099    * @throws IllegalStateException If the stack is empty.
100    */
101   public BeanStore pop() {
102      var f = frames.pollFirst();
103      if (f == null)
104         throw new IllegalStateException("Cannot pop from an empty StackOverlay.");
105      return f;
106   }
107
108   /**
109    * Returns the current depth of the stack.
110    *
111    * @return The number of frames currently on the stack.
112    */
113   public int depth() {
114      return frames.size();
115   }
116
117   /**
118    * Returns the top frame on the stack without removing it.
119    *
120    * @return The top frame, or <jk>null</jk> if the stack is empty.
121    */
122   public BeanStore peek() {
123      return frames.peekFirst();
124   }
125
126   @Override /* BeanStore */
127   public <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> beanType) {
128      return getBean(beanType, null);
129   }
130
131   @Override /* BeanStore */
132   public <T> Optional<T> getBean(Class<T> beanType, String name) {
133      for (var f : frames) {
134         var b = f.getBean(beanType, name);
135         if (b.isPresent())
136            return b;
137      }
138      return opte();
139   }
140
141   @Override /* BeanStore */
142   public <T> Map<String,T> getBeansOfType(Class<T> beanType) {
143      // Walk the stack bottom-to-top so that higher (later-pushed) frames overwrite lower frames
144      // for entries with the same name.  Iterating an iterator gives top-to-bottom; we reverse
145      // that here by snapshotting and walking the reversed iterator.
146      Map<String,T> result = new LinkedHashMap<>();
147      var snapshot = new ArrayList<>(frames);
148      for (var i = snapshot.size() - 1; i >= 0; i--)
149         snapshot.get(i).getBeansOfType(beanType).forEach(result::put);
150      return result;
151   }
152
153   @Override /* BeanStore */
154   public boolean hasBean(Class<?> beanType) {
155      return hasBean(beanType, null);
156   }
157
158   @Override /* BeanStore */
159   public boolean hasBean(Class<?> beanType, String name) {
160      for (var f : frames)
161         if (f.hasBean(beanType, name))
162            return true;
163      return false;
164   }
165
166   @Override /* BeanStore */
167   public <T> Optional<Supplier<T>> getBeanSupplier(Class<T> beanType) {
168      return getBeanSupplier(beanType, null);
169   }
170
171   @Override /* BeanStore */
172   public <T> Optional<Supplier<T>> getBeanSupplier(Class<T> beanType, String name) {
173      for (var f : frames) {
174         var s = f.getBeanSupplier(beanType, name);
175         if (s.isPresent())
176            return s;
177      }
178      return opte();
179   }
180
181   @Override /* BeanStore */
182   public <T> Optional<Class<? extends T>> getBeanType(Class<T> beanType) {
183      for (var f : frames) {
184         var t = f.getBeanType(beanType);
185         if (t.isPresent())
186            return t;
187      }
188      return opte();
189   }
190
191   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
192   public String toString() {
193      // Intentionally avoid recursing into frame.toString() — frames may back-reference the outer
194      // BasicBeanStore that holds this overlay in its overridingParent slot, which would cause
195      // stack overflows via BasicBeanStore.properties().  A flat representation (depth + identity
196      // of each frame) is sufficient for diagnostics.
197      var sb = new StringBuilder("StackOverlay{depth=").append(frames.size()).append(", frames=[");
198      var first = true;
199      for (var f : frames) {
200         if (!first)
201            sb.append(", ");
202         first = false;
203         sb.append(cns(f))
204            .append('@')
205            .append(Integer.toHexString(System.identityHashCode(f)));
206      }
207      return sb.append("]}").toString();
208   }
209}
